steps.title: Put both lines in standard form
steps.content:
Rewrite each equation so the $x$- and $y$-terms are on the left.
$$6x-5y=13y+4$$
$$6x-18y=4$$
$$ty=\frac{3}{2}+3x$$
$$-3x+ty=\frac{3}{2}$$

steps.title: Match the slopes
steps.content: For no solution, the lines must be parallel but distinct. The $x$-coefficient changes from $6$ to $-3$, so the scale factor is $-\frac{1}{2}$.

steps.title: Scale the y coefficient
steps.content:
Apply the same scale factor to the first equation's $y$-coefficient.
$$-18(-\frac{1}{2})=9$$
The constants do not scale the same way because $4(-\frac{1}{2})=-2$, not $\frac{3}{2}$, so the lines are distinct. The answer is 9.
